content.problemStatement:
Recently Chef has decided to make some changes in our beloved **Codechef**. As you know, each problem at Codechef has its memory and time limits. To make problems even more challenging, he decided to measure allocated memory in a different way. Now judge program will be calculating not the maximum memory usage during the execution of all test files, but all the memory ever allocated by the solution program. But as Chef is not that good in algorithms, so he asks you to write a program that will calculate total memory usage of a solution.

So, you are given **N** numbers **M1, , ,MN** representing the measurements of consumed memory (in MBs) for **N** test files. In other terms, it means that on i-th test file, program took **Mi** MBs of memory. Initially, there is no memory allocated for your program. Before running your program on each test file, if the currently allocated memory is more than memory needed for the current test file, then there will be a deallocation of the memory to fit the current program. Also, if there is less than needed memory available, then allocation of memory will happen so as to fit the current program. e.g. Let us say that our program took 10 MBs on current test file. So, assuming if there was 12 MBs memory allocated before running the program on current test file, then there will happen a deallocation of 2 MBs. Assuming if there was 8 MBs memory allocated before running the program on current test file, then there will happen a allocation of 2 MBs.



Calculate the total memory **allocated** for running the solution program on all the **N** test files. Please see third sample for more clarity.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains a single integer N, the number of measurements. The second line contains N space-separated integers, where the ith integer denotes the consumption of memory for the ith test file.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the total memory allocated for running the solution program.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the total memory allocated is 1 because the first measurement is 1 and the second measurement is also 1, so no additional memory is allocated. For the second test case, the total memory allocated is 15 because each measurement is greater than the previous one, so the difference is added to the total. For the third test case, the total memory allocated is 4 because the first measurement is 1, the second measurement is 3 (so 2 is added to the total), and the third measurement is 2 (so no additional memory is allocated).
The total memory allocated is 15 because each measurement is greater than the previous one, so the difference is added to the total.
The total memory allocated is 4 because the first measurement is 1, the second measurement is 3 (so 2 is added to the total), and the third measurement is 2 (so no additional memory is allocated).

solution.approach: The key insight is to calculate the total memory allocated by summing up the differences between the current and previous measurements, but only when the current measurement is greater than the previous one. This is because if the current measurement is less than or equal to the previous one, no additional memory is allocated.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input, as we only use a constant amount of space to store the total memory allocated and the previous measurement.

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N = int(input())
    measurements = list(map(int, input().split()))
    total = measurements[0]
    prev = measurements[0]
    for i in range(1, N):
        curr = measurements[i]
        if curr > prev:
            total += curr - prev
        prev = curr
    print(total)
